title: Platform Prerequisites for Self-Service
author: Martin Fowler
summary:
Martin Fowler explores the essential prerequisites organisations need before offering self-service developer platforms. The article argues that simply building a platform is insufficient — teams must invest in documentation, golden paths, and developer satisfaction measurement.

The piece emphasises that platform engineering should treat developers as customers. Documentation needs to be comprehensive and discoverable, golden paths should reduce cognitive load while preserving flexibility, and satisfaction metrics provide crucial feedback loops for platform improvement.
keyTakeaways: Platform teams should focus on self-service capabilities before scaling
Documentation and golden paths reduce cognitive load for developers
Measuring developer satisfaction is crucial for platform success
Golden paths should be opinionated but not restrictive
Platform engineering requires treating developers as customers
tweetSummary: Platform teams must invest in docs, golden paths, and dev satisfaction before offering self-service — simply building a platform isn't enough.
